violations.text: Cheese was observed without a complete label. PIC discarded cheese. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
Hot water was not available at the hand-washing sink in the produce cutting area. PIC called plumber during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

The produce cutting area lacks a hand-washing sink. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. PIC has work order for sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (produce slicer and produce knife) was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took items out of service to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

Various deli items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
Inspector observed cheese in zip lock bags without labels. Product was embargoed until establishment can obtain labels from manufacture. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

The doors to the outside, located in the front entrance and back storage areas, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Wood is used in the construction of shelf in the meat walk-in cooler. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
The following was observed in poor repair: - Shelf in produce walk-in cooler (rust) - Meat display cooler doors (crack and debris buildup between glass panels). New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the deep freezer chest units have an accumulation of frost/ice. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
3 bay sink faucet in the meat cutting area was observed in poor repair (leak). New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026

The following was observed in poor repair. - Brick wall in storage area (hole). - Floor in meat walk-in cooler (broken tiles). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The walls in the meat walk-in cooler have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

Produce cutting area lacks handwashing sink. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

A container was observed in the hand-washing sink in the meat cutting area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
The hand-washing sinks in the meat cutting was lacking proper handwashing signage. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
Inspector observed cookies from an unapproved source. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
Inspector observed garlic with mold. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Per interview of staff, slicers, bandsaw, and grinder are only washed, rinsed, and sanitized at the end of the day. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as meats and cheeses must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. PIC informed staff during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (meat and veggie slicers and meat knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took out of service to be washed/sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Cheese, milk, garlic, and juice was observed held between 45-53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded or cooled items based on time/temp. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Various deli items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
Inspector observed cheese without proper labels. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The doors to the outside, located in the front entrance and back storage areas, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026
Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
Inspector observed employee uniforms (meat cutting lab coats) stored in the bathroom. PIC informed to not store uniforms in bathroom. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Wood is used in the construction of shelf in the meat walk-in cooler. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

The cutting boards in the meat department are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
Shelf located in the produce walk-in cooler was not in good repair (rust). New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - meat display coolers, - retail shelves, - deep freezer chests (ice buildup), New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
3 Bay sink faucet was observed in poor repair (leak). New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026

The following was observed in poor repair. - ceiling tiles in the retail area (previous water damage) - floor in walk-in produce cooler (need resealing) - brick wall in storage area (holes)The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The ceiling in the meat cutting and produce cutting areas is covered in a material that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable.Inspector observed exposed wood in walk-in produce and meat coolers. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

The floors and walls in the walk-in coolers have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
Inspector observed meat pies and potato balls being sold from an unapproved source. Product Disposed Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

Various cheese products was observed held at ( 43-49 ) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Product Disposed Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

Inspector observed boxes of frozen beef ribs being thawed on floor. Meat within temperature. PIC instructed to remove and informed about safe thawing practices. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

Various food product and beverages were observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler and throughout the retail area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023
Inspector observed chicken being rinsed in the 3 bay sink.PIC instructed of proper procedures for safety. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

The cutting boards in the produce department are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (Quat) sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink the meat department. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

The: 1.fan and walls in milk walk in cooler 2.walls in produce area 3.floors and walls in meat walk in cooler 4.light shields in the produce walk in cooler has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

The: 1.ceilings throughout establishment(water damage) 2.floors throughout establishment(missing tiles) 3.cheese grab n go cooler(ambient temperature 50 degrees Fahrenheit) 4.bottom of meat cooler door area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed around the perimeter of the back outside area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023
